overall_justification: While the newspaper reports on both sides of the Caixa de Conversão debate, its overall tone and framing lean towards the Orthodox perspective.  The article questioning Joaquim Murlinho's potential resignation frames his opposition to the Caixa and the Convênio de Taubaté negatively, portraying it as a desertion of duty and prioritizing personal loyalties over national interest. This implicit criticism of the expansionist position, combined with the lack of equally strong endorsements of the expansionist view, suggests a cautious, if not outright critical, stance towards a significant change to the monetary system.

supporting_evidence.quote: Resignando o seu posto de senador, S. Ex. resigna o seu direito de voto e a sua auctoridade de representante da nação. Verdadeiramente não é uma resignação, é uma deserção.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote, from a prominent article discussing a key political figure's potential resignation over the Caixa, strongly implies a criticism of those opposing the measure and frames their actions as abandoning their responsibilities.

supporting_evidence.quote: Se as medidas de que se trata são, na opinião de S. Ex., contrarias aos interesses do paiz, dados os altivos precedentes de S. Ex., não se comprehende como esses interesses ficam subalternos ás conveniencias de um grupo de amigos politicos.
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This passage further reinforces the negative framing of opposition to the Caixa, suggesting that personal political alliances are being prioritized over the national good.
